SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.rupture

Class VesselRuptureAnalyzer

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.rupture.VesselRuptureAnalyzer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class VesselRuptureAnalyzer extends Object implements Serializable
Thin-wall vessel rupture analyzer that tracks the von Mises wall stress against the temperature-derated allowable tensile strength of the material throughout a fire or blowdown transient.

At each time step the internal pressure and wall (metal) temperature give a von Mises equivalent stress (via VesselRuptureCalculator.vonMisesStress(double, double, double)) which is compared with the allowable rupture stress obtained by derating the ambient tensile strength with the MaterialStrengthCurve. Rupture is predicted at the first crossover where the stress reaches the allowable strength; the crossover time is found by linear interpolation of the margin between the bracketing steps. This is the time-marching equivalent of the Birk/Moodie fire-engulfment validation cases and complements the strain-rate pipe rupture variant already in this package.

References: Birk, A.M. and Cunningham, M.H. (1996), J. Loss Prev. Process Ind. 9(3); Moodie et al. (1988), J. Hazard. Mater. 20; API 521 §4.3.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • VesselRuptureAnalyzer

      public VesselRuptureAnalyzer(double innerRadiusM, double wallThicknessM, MaterialStrengthCurve material)
      Creates a vessel rupture analyzer.
      Parameters:
      innerRadiusM - vessel inner radius in m; must be positive
      wallThicknessM - wall thickness in m; must be positive
      material - temperature-dependent material strength curve; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is invalid

  • Method Details

    • setTensileStrengthFactor

      public VesselRuptureAnalyzer setTensileStrengthFactor(double tensileStrengthFactor)
      Sets the tensile-strength utilization factor applied when computing the allowable rupture stress.
      Parameters:
      tensileStrengthFactor - fraction of the derated tensile strength taken as allowable (0 to 1]; must be positive and at most one
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if tensileStrengthFactor is out of range

    • wallStressPa

      public double wallStressPa(double internalPressurePa)
      Computes the von Mises wall stress for a single state.
      Parameters:
      internalPressurePa - internal pressure in Pa absolute; must be non-negative
      Returns:
      von Mises stress in Pa

    • allowableStressPa

      public double allowableStressPa(double metalTemperatureK)
      Computes the allowable rupture stress for a single metal temperature.
      Parameters:
      metalTemperatureK - wall (metal) temperature in K; must be positive
      Returns:
      allowable rupture stress in Pa

    • analyze

      public VesselRuptureAnalyzer.VesselRuptureResult analyze(double[] timeS, double[] internalPressurePa, double[] metalTemperatureK)
      Analyzes a transient history and determines whether and when the wall ruptures.
      Parameters:
      timeS - time stamps in s; strictly increasing, at least one element
      internalPressurePa - internal pressure history in Pa absolute, same length as timeS
      metalTemperatureK - wall (metal) temperature history in K, same length as timeS
      Returns:
      a VesselRuptureAnalyzer.VesselRuptureResult with the per-step stress/strength history and the rupture verdict
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the arrays are null, empty or of differing length

    • getMaterial

      public MaterialStrengthCurve getMaterial()
      Gets the material strength curve used by this analyzer.
      Returns:
      the material strength curve
